notes_md:
**Mai mốt** means *later on* or *sometime in the future*, but not at a specific time. It feels casual and a bit vague — like “one of these days.”

It’s usually placed at the beginning of a sentence to set a future time idea.  

You don’t need **sẽ**, but they often appear together to clearly mark the future.  

Close synonym: **sau này** (more like “in the future” in a longer-term sense). Compared to **tương lai**, *mai mốt* is less formal and more conversational.
notes:
Mai mốt means later on or sometime in the future, but not at a specific time. It feels casual and a bit vague — like “one of these days.”

It’s usually placed at the beginning of a sentence to set a future time idea.  

You don’t need sẽ, but they often appear together to clearly mark the future.  

Close synonym: sau này (more like “in the future” in a longer-term sense). Compared to tương lai, mai mốt is less formal and more conversational.
exampleSentences: Mai mốt anh sẽ dẫn em đi ăn món này nhé.
Chị nhớ giữ gìn sức khỏe để mai mốt còn đi du lịch cùng nhau.
Em hãy cố gắng học tốt để mai mốt có nhiều cơ hội hơn.
exampleSentencesTranslated: In the future I will take you to eat this dish.
Remember to take care of your health so in the future we can travel together.
Try to study well so in the future you will have more opportunities.
